summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates environment credentials and host metadata to attacker-controlled webhook
details: package.json declares `preinstall: node index.js`, which runs automatically on `npm install`. The script enumerates `process.env` for credential-shaped variables (NPM_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AZURE_*, DOCKER_PASSWORD, and any key containing SECRET/TOKEN/PASSWORD/KEY/CREDENTIAL) and bundles them with host identifiers (os.hostname, os.userInfo, cwd, platform, arch), memory stats, CI provider detection, and a snapshot of the top processes via `execSync('ps -eo pid,pcpu,pmem,user,comm --sort=-pcpu | head -n 8')`. The collected payload is POSTed over HTTPS to a hardcoded Pipedream webhook at https://eov0bmnid410yqf.m.pipedream.net (index.js line 6, sent via https.request at line 114). A comment self-labels the behavior as a 'pentest canary,' but the package is published to the public registry under a design-system name and any installer becomes the victim — there is no consent mechanism and the destination is author-controlled. This is a textbook install-time credential-harvesting exfiltrator.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
